title: Algebra Terms
text: A mathematical statement that two expressions are equal is called an {{blank-1}}. The part of an equation that contains a variable and its coefficient is a {{blank-2}}. To solve for x, you need to {{blank-3}} the equation. When you move a term to the other side of the equals sign, you must {{blank-4}} it. An equation of the form y = mx + b represents a {{blank-5}}.
